scope: Pass B full-surface expansion (measure-first probe): every documented Gmail API v1 REST endpoint (79 total, per the live Discovery document at https://www.googleapis.com/discovery/v1/apis/gmail/v1/rest, revision confirmed 2026-07-03) is enumerated below. 10 practical GET list/detail resources are implemented as streams; every expressible mutation (create/update/patch/delete/send/modify/trash/untrash) is implemented as a writes.json action (24 actions). Detail-by-id GETs for resources already covered by their list stream, push-notification control endpoints, binary attachment/certificate payloads, batch endpoints whose one-record-per-write model is redundant with the equivalent looped single-record write, and the narrow admin-only client-side-encryption (CSE) key management surface are excluded with real categories below -- out_of_scope is no longer used as a blanket bucket.

excluded.reason: single-message detail GET returns the identical Message resource shape the messages list stream already paginates through in full; no additional record data would be gained by a second, non-paginated per-id fetch

bulk variant of modify_message over an ids[] array; the engine's write dialect is one-request-per-record, so the same outcome is reached by calling modify_message once per id -- no distinct record shape or mutation this connector cannot already express
bulk variant of delete_message over an ids[] array; reachable by calling delete_message once per id under the engine's one-request-per-record write model
returns base64url-encoded raw attachment bytes, not a syncable structured record
single-thread detail GET (with its embedded messages[]) returns the identical Thread resource shape the threads list stream already paginates through in full
single-draft detail GET returns the identical Draft resource shape the drafts list stream already paginates through in full
single-label detail GET returns the identical Label resource shape the labels list stream already returns in full (labels.list is unpaginated and already returns every label)
registers a Cloud Pub/Sub push-notification subscription for mailbox changes; a control-plane side effect, not a record read or a syncable mutation of user data
cancels the watch() push-notification subscription; the paired control-plane action to the excluded watch() endpoint above
single-filter detail GET returns the identical Filter resource shape the filters list stream already returns in full (filters.list is unpaginated)
single-alias detail GET returns the identical SendAs resource shape the send_as list stream already returns in full (sendAs.list is unpaginated)

S/MIME certificate management requires the narrow https://mail.google.com/ full-mailbox scope beyond gmail.settings.basic/gmail.readonly and returns certificate material (pkcs12/private key password), not general syncable business data
same S/MIME elevated-scope certificate surface as the smimeInfo list endpoint above
uploads a PKCS#12 certificate blob under the elevated S/MIME scope; a binary certificate-provisioning action, not a general reverse-ETL record write
same S/MIME elevated-scope certificate surface as the smimeInfo list endpoint above
same S/MIME elevated-scope certificate surface as the smimeInfo list endpoint above
single-delegate detail GET returns the identical Delegate resource shape the delegates list stream already returns in full (delegates.list is unpaginated)
single-address detail GET returns the identical ForwardingAddress resource shape the forwarding_addresses list stream already returns in full (forwardingAddresses.list is unpaginated)

reads back the single global auto-forwarding settings singleton this bundle already writes via update_auto_forwarding; a get-after-write confirmation read, not an independent syncable record collection (there is exactly one auto-forwarding setting per mailbox, no list/paginated shape to model as a stream)
reads back the single global vacation-responder settings singleton this bundle already writes via update_vacation; same get-after-write reasoning as autoForwarding above
reads back the single global display-language settings singleton this bundle already writes via update_language
reads back the single global IMAP settings singleton this bundle already writes via update_imap
reads back the single global POP settings singleton this bundle already writes via update_pop

Client-Side Encryption (CSE) is a Google Workspace Enterprise Plus / Education Plus add-on gated behind organization-level admin enablement and the elevated gmail.settings.basic+CSE-specific setup; not a general-availability surface reachable by an ordinary OAuth refresh-token grant
same CSE elevated-scope/Workspace-add-on surface as the identities list endpoint above
same CSE elevated-scope/Workspace-add-on surface; provisions an encryption identity, not a general business record
same CSE elevated-scope/Workspace-add-on surface as the identities list endpoint above
same CSE elevated-scope/Workspace-add-on surface as the identities list endpoint above
same CSE elevated-scope/Workspace-add-on surface; returns encryption key-pair metadata, not general business data
same CSE elevated-scope/Workspace-add-on surface as the keypairs list endpoint above
uploads a PKCS#7/PEM key-pair blob under the CSE Workspace-add-on surface; a binary key-material provisioning action
same CSE elevated-scope/Workspace-add-on surface as the keypairs list endpoint above
same CSE elevated-scope/Workspace-add-on surface as the keypairs list endpoint above

irreversibly and permanently destroys an encryption key pair (Google's own naming), rendering any mail encrypted with it permanently unreadable; an organization-admin-only destructive operation, not an ordinary reverse-ETL write
